_source: BYTE-GROUNDED catalog decoded 2026-07-10 from the ORIGINAL US PSX disc EXE (SLUS_012.79, name/desc glyph strings via catalog table RAM 0x80019770) cross-checked against the PC port table Dino2.exe VA 0x704260 (same 12-byte records, same flags/iconUV, JP glyph strings at ptr+0/ptr+4). Authored RE knowledge (offsets + names as identifiers), no game bytes. Supersedes the earlier guide-sourced placeholder file. See docs/decisions/dc2/ITEMS-KEYITEMS-RE-PLAN.md and K75.
_encoding_note: US SLUS names are word-per-glyph strings: glyph/2 -> 1..26=A..Z, 27..52=a..z, 0=space, 0x6a+2n=digit n, 0x82='?', 0x90='-', 0x9a='.', 0x9c=',', 0x9e=apostrophe; 0xf000=line break, 0x4000=end. PC port carries the same table with JAPANESE katakana/kanji glyph strings (0x7244c4 region).
_id_spaces: TWO DISTINCT id spaces. (1) THIS catalog id = the INVENTORY item id (record scene_mgr+0x10bc + slot*12, byte+1; equipped main/sub at scene_mgr+0x10b4/+0x10b5, sub stored as id|0x10; ITEM.DAT icon block index == this id, loader 0x403ed0). (2) The op-0x31 room-script byte (data/dc2/item-placements.json, ids 1..9) is the AOT SAT-1 VM-THREAD SLOT, param = routine index -- NOT this catalog id (K77/K129). Catalog-bearing room sources are normalized separately in data/dc2/item-sources.json.

catalog.name: Shockwave
catalog.note: name string prefixed by two blank lines in the source table

catalog.name: Machete Improved
catalog.note: native transform target: EXE 0x40ceaf rewrites inventory id 0x11 -> 0x17

catalog.name: Large Stungun (improved)
catalog.note: native transform target: EXE 0x40cf57 rewrites inventory id 0x12 -> 0x18; US name string identical to 0x12

catalog.name: Key plate
catalog.note: ids 0x25-0x2a are six Key plate rows sharing one name/desc string (US ptr 0x8008c528)

catalog.name: Signal Bullet
catalog.note: key-item duplicate of sub 0x19 (same display name, capital B)

_owner_flags_note: flags word (rec+0xA, PSX-truth per data/dc2/weapon-catalog.json): 0x01 Regina / 0x02 Dylan / 0x04 David owner bits + 0x08 MAIN / 0x10 SUB class + 0x20 wide-icon. Weapon rows carry the weapon-catalog.json values; item rows (0x1a+) are flags 0x0000.

_sat1_triggers._source: decode_script op-0x31 census: 168 SAT-1 physical VM triggers in 54 rooms (data/dc2/item-placements.json).
_sat1_triggers._semantics: block+0x04 is the VM-thread slot and block+0x00 is the slot-5 routine index. Neither is an item type, quantity, catalog id, or rewrite lever. The old empirical item-name/type mapping is retracted by K129.
_sat1_triggers._catalog_sources: Use data/dc2/item-sources.json: op-0x35 SAT-5 TAKE-ITEM and only kind-1 op-0x2C GiveItem rows carry catalog-bearing item operands.

keyItemGrantSites._source: Legacy grouped view of literal op-0x1c/op-0x33 SetFlag(9,id,1) writes (K101). It is no longer the complete acquisition-source model: data/dc2/item-sources.json preserves every individual grant/clear site plus op-0x35 TAKE-ITEM, kind-1 op-0x2C GiveItem, shop and native classifications. value:=0 rows are clears, not grants.
keyItemGrantSites._runtime_or_native: Absence of a group-9 :=1 write is not an absence of a static item source. K129 attributes previously unmodelled keys through op-0x35, kind-1 op-0x2C, or the native shop surface; consult item-sources.json for the tested disposition of every supported and unsupported record.
keyItemGrantSites._door_gate_note: K119/K121 supersede K107: the corpus has exactly one cross-room held-item gate. ST10D->ST10F commits only while flag(9,0x2e) owns the Gas Mask. Other locked doors use equipped-subweapon classes or story/event flags; no second held-key door gate is proven.

dinoFiles._source: guide-sourced (FAQ); dino files route to the log, not inventory -- no catalog rows
